d-a-child-recusal-2025-12-09.lesson: Pro-se family-law filers in UK appellate courts are now flagged for AI-fabricated authorities; verify every cite on opposing submissions.

omkara-assets-reconstruction-private-lim.lesson: Indian Supreme Court is openly warning counsel on AI-fabricated authorities mid-hearing; assume zero tolerance in apex courts.

s-peggie-v-fife-health-dr-b-upton-2025-1.lesson: Even judges are being accused of AI hallucinations; if a quote you filed is challenged, verify and correct before the bench beats you to it.

barrette-v-wevers-2025-12-08.lesson: Alberta courts are imposing enhanced costs against pro-se appellants for AI-fabricated authorities and regulations.

chu-v-darmali-2025-12-08.lesson: Australian Federal Circuit is flagging generative-AI submissions filled with inapt authorities; expect courts to discount whole briefs.

onyinye-udokporo-v-enrich-international-.lesson: UK IPO tribunals are catching AI-fabricated trademark citations and altered quotations; IP disputes are no safe harbor.

balogun-v-wentworth-standard-condominium.lesson: Ontario condo tribunals openly label AI-hallucinated jurisdictional cites and refuse to consider them.

carrington-v-tafe-queensland-2025-12-05.lesson: Neutral-citation numbers that point to different cases are a tell — cross-check every QIRC/neutral citation against the actual decision.

jvec-v-smd-2025-12-04.lesson: Saskatchewan courts are treating 'I used Google' as equivalent to AI misuse — consumer tools get no leniency.

n-2509827-2025-12-03.lesson: French administrative tribunals are now documenting AI fabrications in pro-se filings; precedent is spreading across civil-law jurisdictions.

taiwo-v-homelets-of-bath-limited-2025-12.lesson: UK High Court imposed an adverse costs order for bogus AI cites even at nominal amount — the order itself is the reputational penalty.

husni-al-qawasmi-association-et-al-v-zia.lesson: Israeli Supreme Court now issues monetary sanctions for AI-fabricated citations even against pro-se litigants.

luis-alberto-contreras-fandinoe-2025-12-.lesson: Colombian appellate courts are quashing judgments and referring judges to discipline for AI-fabricated authorities and doctrinal works — judicial conduct now in the crosshairs.

avard-v-mornington-peninsula-sc-2025-12-.lesson: Victorian CAT is proceeding past AI cites by applying the real underlying authority — courts will no longer be derailed by fake names.

heneghan-v-the-owners-strata-plan-187-20.lesson: BC Civil Resolution Tribunal is explicitly labeling three-cite fabrications as 'AI hallucinations' in reasons — the record follows the litigant.

unknown-criminal-case-2025-12-01.lesson: Spanish criminal proceedings now allege AI hallucinations against prosecutors and judges — government lawyers are not exempt.

wang-fengxi-re-2025-12-01.lesson: Ontario SCJ admonishments for AI fabrications are stacking up in reported reasons — the public record compounds over time.

internet-sciences-inc-v-cnsx-markets-inc.lesson: Ontario capital-markets tribunal is warning pro-se filers for fabricated and misrepresented legal norms — regulators have joined the enforcement wave.

obermann-v-spring-financial-inc-2025-11-.lesson: BC CRT is refusing to engage AI-hallucinated arguments on the merits — fabrication now causes your argument to disappear, not just get corrected.

mertz-mertz-no-3-2025-11-28.lesson: Australian family courts are now pairing AUD 10K costs orders with bar referrals when counsel files unverified AI authorities — professional jeopardy is real.
